meta.title: Asteraid — Professional Asterisk PBX Management
meta.description: Web platform for Asterisk administration: real-time station monitoring, visual dialplan editor, CDR, SSH terminal and Active Directory.

hero.subheadline: Professional web platform for Asterisk PBX administration: real-time monitoring, visual dialplan editor, call history and SSH terminal right in the browser.

stats.headline: Everything you need to manage PBX

features.subheadline: From real-time monitoring to SSH terminal — everything a telephony engineer needs.

items.title: Dialplan Rule Testing
items.description: Simulate a call flow through the dialplan directly from the Visual Dialplan Editor, using the administrator's phone to emulate an incoming call.
items: Run call simulation from the editor without leaving the UI
Use the admin's phone as the emulated incoming call source
Create a full test environment to debug dialplan logic
Validate routing rules before applying to production
Catch errors before they reach live infrastructure

items.title: Multilingual Support
items.description: Multilingual support for voice files at the peer level. Each user can hear IVR prompts and system messages in their own language.
items: Per-peer language assignment for IVR prompts and system messages
Supported languages: English, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Russian, Swedish
Support for custom dictionaries to display GUI elements in any language

items.title: Version History & VM Boot Mode
items.description: Full version history support for the entire configuration. A backup snapshot is created every time the "Apply" button is pressed.
items: Automatic snapshot on every configuration apply
Browse and restore any previous configuration state

items.title: Real-time Monitoring
items.description: Via WebSocket the platform continuously receives and displays live metrics from all connected Asterisk stations.

items.title: Multi-Station Management
items.description: Asteraid lets you add any number of Asterisk servers and manage them centrally.

items.title: Visual Dialplan Editor
items.description: Unique feature: build call routing via drag-and-drop block diagram powered by ReactFlow.
items: Create and edit routing Rules templates

items.title: Call History (CDR)
items.description: Full call log via ODBC connection to the CDR database with powerful filters.

items.title: Extensions, Trunks & Queues
items.description: Full management of Asterisk telephony objects: extensions, trunks, templates, queues.

items.title: Users & Roles
items.description: Flexible role model with four access levels. Each role gets its own dashboard.

items.title: Active Directory Integration
items.description: Seamless user import from the corporate LDAP directory.
items: Map AD groups to Asteraid roles

items.title: SSH Web Terminal
items.description: Full-featured SSH terminal right in the browser powered by xterm.js.

items.title: Firewall Management
items.description: Built-in firewall at station level with ipset support.
items: Allow / deny rules by IP / CIDR

items.title: Config File Editor
items.description: Built-in Monaco editor (like VS Code) for direct Asterisk config file editing.

items.title: SSL Certificates
items.description: Certificate management for secure telephony with metadata display.

items.title: Action Log
items.description: Complete history of all system changes with diff view.

roles.subheadline: 4 role-based access levels. Each user sees only what they need for their work.

items.name: Administrator
items.description: Full access to all platform sections

items.name: Supervisor
items.description: Call statistics and team management

items.name: User
items.description: Personal employee account

items.name: Security
items.description: Specialized security dashboard

howItWorks.headline: How it works
howItWorks.subheadline: From installation to a working platform — the initialization wizard walks you through all steps in 5 minutes.
howItWorks.wizardDesc: Step-by-step wizard: Admin → Connection → Groups → CDR → Certificates → Finished

steps.title: Installation
steps.description: Deploy Asteraid on your server. The step-by-step initialization wizard guides you through all settings in 5 minutes.

steps.title: Connect Stations
steps.description: Add your Asterisk servers by hostname. Asteraid connects via SSH and starts monitoring automatically.

steps.title: Set Up Users
steps.description: Import employees from Active Directory or create them manually. Assign roles — each gets their own dashboard.

steps.title: Manage Telephony
steps.description: Trunks, extensions, queues, dialplans — all accessible from the browser. The visual routing editor works without Asterisk syntax knowledge.

benefits.subheadline: Built by IT engineers for IT engineers. No marketing fluff — just concrete solutions to concrete problems.

items.title: Single Portal
items.description: Manage all company Asterisk servers from one place without switching between SSH sessions and different interfaces.

items.title: Lower Entry Barrier
items.description: Visual dialplan editor and ready-made templates lower the entry barrier — no Asterisk expert needed for every click.

items.title: Real-time Control
items.description: See infrastructure status here and now — via WebSocket, not cron scripts.

items.title: Full History
items.description: Audit log records every change: who, what, when and how it was changed. With diff view.

items.title: Enterprise Integration
items.description: Active Directory, SSL certificates, ODBC CDR databases — full integration with your infrastructure.

items.title: Security
items.description: Built-in Firewall, TLS certificates, encryption and role-based permissions — security out of the box.

items.title: SSH in Browser
items.description: No need to open PuTTY. Full SSH terminal right in a browser tab.

items.title: Scalability
items.description: Works with one station and a cluster. Adding new servers takes a couple of clicks.

beforeAfter.subheadline: The real difference between managing Asterisk manually and with Asteraid.
beforeAfter.summaryBefore: Scattered tools · SSH sessions · manual config · no visibility
beforeAfter.summaryAfter: One interface · automation · templates · full control

items.before: SSH into each server separately, juggling different IPs and credentials
items.after: All Asterisk servers in one web interface — switch stations in one click

items.before: Edit .conf files in terminal — one typo can break the entire PBX
items.after: Visual editor with templates and Monaco syntax highlighting, no broken configs

items.before: Raw SQL queries against the CDR database or third-party reporting tools
items.after: Built-in CDR: filters by type/number, audio playback, one-click CSV/XLSX export

items.before: Write Asterisk dialplan syntax by hand — requires deep expert knowledge
items.after: Drag-and-drop visual builder generates real dialplan automatically

items.before: Edit sip.conf manually for every extension change, no bulk operations
items.after: Web UI with templates, bulk CSV import, and Active Directory sync

items.before: No record of who changed what — debugging is a guessing game
items.after: Full audit log: actor, timestamp, diff view of before/after values

items.before: iptables commands over SSH, manual certificate files, no central view
items.after: Built-in Firewall manager with ipset and SSL certificate dashboard

items.before: CLI tools (asterisk -r), no dashboards, no alerts
items.after: Live dashboard via WebSocket: RAM, trunks, extensions, active channels

systemRequirements.subheadline: Minimum hardware and network requirements for deploying Asteraid.

rows.component: Storage (OS with Docker images and containers only. For user data, such as backups, audio recordings, CDRs, etc., it is recommended to use separate disks.)

rows.virtual: Ethernet adapter in bridge mode (NAT not recommended)

systemRequirements.noteText: These are minimum requirements. Production environments typically require more resources. 2 CPU Core and 8 GB of RAM should be sufficient in most cases.

networkItems.label: Firewall Access:
networkItems.text: HTTP/HTTPS access to Asteraid repositories must be allowed

networkItems.label: Proxy Configuration:
networkItems.text: Configure proxy settings if behind a corporate proxy

techStack.subheadline: Production-ready stack with no experimental dependencies.

screenshots.subheadline: Real screenshots from the live system — no mockups, no stock photos.

items.title: Visual Dialplan Editor
items.description: Build complex call routing without touching a single line of Asterisk config. Drag-and-drop blocks connect visually and generate real dialplan automatically.
items: Drag-and-drop routing blocks: Queue, Hangup, Sound, Menu, Outbound
Visual arrows between blocks — see the full call flow at a glance
Generates valid Asterisk dialplan in real time
Switch between visual and JSON/text view

items.title: PBX Configuration File Editor
items.description: A full Monaco-based code editor (the same engine as VS Code) for direct Asterisk config file management — with syntax highlighting and file switching.
items: Browse and edit all Asterisk .conf files in-browser
Switch between system and client config files

items.title: Backup & Data Protection
items.description: Automated scheduled backups with AES encryption. Configure interval, time, and path — then let Asteraid handle the rest.
items: Flexible schedule: every day, 3 days, week, 2 weeks, or month
Set backup time and target path
Encryption key management for database DUMP

items.title: Full Audit Trail
items.description: Every change made in the system is logged with the user, timestamp, entity, and action type. Filter by user or entity, search by changes.
items: Shows which user performed each action
Entity type: Rules, Dialplan blocks, Extensions, etc.
Filter by period, user, and entity

items.title: Before / After Change View
items.description: Click any log entry to see the exact diff of what was changed — side-by-side view of the previous and new configuration state.
items: Full config context around each change
Works for all entity types in the system

faq.headline: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subheadline: Didn't find the answer? Write to us — we'll respond within a business day.

items.question: Does Asteraid work with multiple Asterisk servers?
items.answer: Yes, Asteraid is designed for multi-station management. You add any number of servers and manage them centrally from one interface.

items.question: Do I need Asterisk knowledge to use the platform?
items.answer: Basic knowledge helps, but the visual dialplan editor and configuration templates significantly lower the entry barrier. Most tasks are done through the graphical interface.

items.question: Is Active Directory supported?
items.answer: Yes, Asteraid supports connecting to multiple AD controllers with group mapping to roles (Administrator, Supervisor, User). Users are imported with their data.

items.question: How are call records stored?
items.answer: Asteraid connects to your existing CDR database via ODBC. Records stay where they always were — nothing is migrated.

items.question: Is there a mobile version?
items.answer: The interface is responsive. Primary use is designed for desktop, but key sections are accessible from a tablet.

items.question: How is security ensured?
items.answer: JWT authentication, bcrypt password hashing, role-based access control (RBAC), built-in Firewall with ipset support, TLS certificate management, encryption key management.

cta.subheadline: Request a demo or deploy Asteraid yourself — the initialization wizard will guide you in 5 minutes.

footer.description: Professional web platform for Asterisk PBX administration.
